title: AgentOps Verdict Ledger v1
description: Append-only per-iteration record of each GOALS.md directive's scenario verdict, written by `ao goals measure` (F2) and read by the F5.2 re-steer policy engine to detect failure streaks and cooldowns. An iteration is exactly one completed `ao goals measure` run that recorded a scenario verdict for the directive (see docs/adr/ADR-0006-re-steer-policy-and-mutation-safety.md). The runtime artifact lives at .agents/goals/verdict-ledger.json (untracked); this schema and its fixtures are tracked.

generated_at.description: RFC3339 timestamp when this ledger file was last rewritten.

records.description: Append-only ledger records in iteration order (oldest first). Iteration records and cooldown records are interleaved on the timeline and discriminated by the `record_type` field.

iteration_record.description: One completed `ao goals measure` iteration's scenario verdict for one directive.

record_type.description: Discriminator: a per-iteration scenario verdict record.

directive_id.description: Stable GOALS.md directive ID this verdict belongs to. Never a display number.

run_timestamp.description: RFC3339 timestamp of the `ao goals measure` run that produced this verdict.

scenario_verdict.description: Per-directive scenario verdict. `unknown` (no scenarios linked) breaks any in-progress failure streak; `skip` likewise does not advance a streak.

scenario_satisfaction.description: Aggregate scenario satisfaction for the directive (0.0-1.0).

scenario_count.description: Number of scenarios linked to the directive at iteration time.

evaluated_count.description: Number of linked scenarios that were actually evaluated this iteration.

run_id.description: Optional RPI run identifier that triggered the measure run.

cooldown_record.description: A re-steer proposal or application recorded against a directive. Its position in the records timeline marks the start of the cooldown window (the directive is skipped by the re-steer engine for the next `cooldown_iterations` iterations).

directive_id.description: Stable GOALS.md directive ID the re-steer event targeted.

run_timestamp.description: RFC3339 timestamp when the re-steer proposal/application was recorded.

cooldown_kind.description: Whether the mutation was proposed (recommendation-only) or applied to GOALS.md.

mutation_type.description: The re-steer mutation type recorded. Matches the re-steer-policy.v1 mutation enum.

note.description: Optional human-readable rationale for the re-steer event.
